title: EEG: Simon Conflict w/ Reinforcement + Cabergoline Challenge
description: This dataset comprises electroencephalography (EEG) recordings from 110 healthy participants performing a Simon conflict task with reinforcement-based cost manipulation. The study includes two components: a larger observational cohort (n=83) and a double-blind pharmacological intervention study (n=27) examining the effects of Cabergoline (1.25 mg dopamine agonist) on conflict processing and decision-making. Data were collected during 2012-2013 and provide insights into neural mechanisms of cognitive control and reward-based learning.
methods_description: EEG was recorded while participants performed a Simon conflict task with reinforcement manipulation. Study 1 included 80 healthy participants plus 5 placebo sessions from a pilot drug study (total n=83). Study 2 was a double-blind design with 30 participants (27 completers) receiving Cabergoline 1.25 mg or placebo. Some participants completed two sessions. Data were collected at the Laboratory for Neural Computation & Cognition at Brown University circa 2012-2013.
